chore: remove neighbour/distance peer pool distinction from DHT connectivity (#7702)

DHT connectivity maintained two separate peer pools — a `neighbours`
pool (originally distance-ordered) and a `random_pool` — which became
functionally identical after XOR-distance selection was removed. This PR
collapses them into a single unified pool.

## DHT Connectivity (`comms/dht/src/connectivity/mod.rs`)

- Removed `neighbours: Vec<NodeId>` field; all managed peers live in
`random_pool`
- Total pool capacity = `num_neighbouring_nodes + num_random_nodes`
(config fields preserved for backward compat)
- Removed functions: `refresh_neighbour_pool`,
`refresh_neighbour_pool_if_required`, `num_connected_neighbours`,
`insert_neighbour`, `redial_neighbours_as_required`,
`fetch_neighbouring_peers`
- `replace_pool_peer` now has a single branch (random pool only)
- `log_status` reports a single peer pool instead of separate
neighbour/random pools
- Ticker loop drops the `refresh_neighbour_pool_if_required` call;
single `refresh_random_pool_if_required` handles everything

## Config & Builder (`config.rs`, `builder.rs`)

- Updated doc comments on `num_neighbouring_nodes` / `num_random_nodes`
to drop "neighbour" framing — they now describe pool partitioning, not
topology
- `DhtConnectivityConfig` comment updated accordingly

## Tests (`connectivity/test.rs`)

- `insert_neighbour` → `insert_into_pool` (tests unified
`insert_random_peer` cap behaviour)
- `added_neighbours` → `added_pool_peers`
- `replace_peer_when_peer_goes_offline` rewritten to not rely on
specific peer indices (random selection makes index-based assertions
fragile)
- `initialize` checks dial count rather than specific peer IDs

Expand Imperator/CK3 mod detection via configurables instead of hardcoded logic (#2990)

Mod detection was entirely hardcoded (`StartsWith("The Fallen Eagle")`,
etc.) and file-tweaker blocks were gated on hardcoded boolean properties
per mod. This makes adding new mod support require C# changes. Replaces
both with configurable-driven detection.

## New configurable format

`configurables/ck3_mods.txt` and `configurables/imperator_mods.txt`
define detection rules:

```
tfe = {
    name_regex = { "^The Fallen Eagle" }
    id = { "ugc_2243307127.mod" }
}
```

A flag is activated if any loaded mod's name matches any regex **or**
its path ends with any listed ID. Both fields are optional.

## Key changes

- **`CommonUtils/ModDefinition.cs`** — New `ModDefinition` and
`ModDefinitionsReader` classes; parses the configurable format, regex
compiled with 1s timeout (ReDoS protection)
- **`configurables/ck3_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `tfe`, `wtwsms`,
`roa`, `aep`
- **`configurables/imperator_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `invictus`,
`invictus_1_7`, `terra_indomita`
- **`Configuration.cs`** — `DetectSpecificCK3Mods` now loads
`ck3_mods.txt`; `FallenEagleEnabled` etc. become computed properties
from a `HashSet<string> activeCK3ModFlags`; `GetCK3ModFlags()` builds
dynamically from loaded definitions so any new flag in the file
automatically propagates to Liquid templates.
`DetectSpecificImperatorMods` moved here from `Imperator/World.cs`;
`InvictusDetected`, `Invictus1_7Detected`, `TerraIndomitaDetected` are
now computed properties on `Configuration`; `AddImperatorModFlag()`
added for fallback detection from save data; `GetLiquidVariables()` now
includes Imperator mod flags with an `ir_` prefix (e.g. `ir_invictus`,
`ir_terra_indomita`)
- **`Imperator/World.cs`** — Calls
`config.DetectSpecificImperatorMods(UsableMods)` after mod loading;
save-data fallback detection (global flag `is_playing_invictus`, country
`unification_points` variable) handled via
`config.AddImperatorModFlag()`
- **`CK3/World.cs`** — Uses `config.InvictusDetected`,
`config.Invictus1_7Detected`, `config.TerraIndomitaDetected` instead of
reading from `irWorld`
- **`FileTweaker.cs`** — Hardcoded `if (config.FallenEagleEnabled)`
blocks replaced with a loop over `config.GetActiveCK3ModFlags()`,
loading `removable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` /
`replaceable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` when they exist — new mods need
only a configurable entry and the block files, no C# changes

## Backward compatibility

Existing boolean properties and flag keys (`tfe`, `vanilla`, etc.) are
preserved. `ThrowUserErrorExceptionForUnsupportedModCombinations` is
unchanged.

> <issue_description>Original Concept:

> > As far as I'm aware, the converter only really checks what Imperator
mods are being used to know what province mappings to use. While there
might not be too many cases where knowing exactly what mods were used in
Imperator would be relevant for how CK3 is setup, I figured there
*might* be a mod I couldn't think of, or one that gets made in the
future, where using it in Imperator might have you want CK3 to be
handled a little differently compared to normal (one such case might be
knowing whether the `ba-to-imperator` converter was used). So, since I
was going to mention expanding the CK3 mod liquid dictionary, I figured
I'd at least mention checking Imperator mods while I was at it. The
process regarding Imperator mods would pretty much be the same as CK3
mods below.
> > 
> > The gist of this would be probably having some configurable files
setup that list mods from Imperator and/or CK3 with ways for the
converter to recognize they are being used. For example, the converter
currently checks if TFE is being used if one of the mods chosen has its
name start with "The Fallen Eagle", which then has `tfe` be added to the
CK3 mods liquid dictionary. This could potentially be changed to
something in the configurable like:
> ```
> tfe = {
>    name = { "The Fallen Eagle" }
>    id = { ugc_2243307127.mod } # Steam Workshop ID
> }
> ```
> > I don't know what the best way to format it would be, but the idea
is that if any of the mods loaded meet either of those conditions, the
`tfe` flag gets added to the liquid dictionary, which could then be used
wherever its needed. The converter only currently checks the main
compatible, overhaul mods since those are needed to know which province
mappings will be used. But, there are potentially other mods where
knowing they will be loaded would be useful, like RICE or Cultures
Expanded, which add a lot of relevant content that might need to be
modified to better fit in a converted game. This could also be expanded
to better be used in other parts of the converter, like
`converter_cultures/faiths.txt` INVALIDATED block, the
`cultural_pillars` MOD_DEPENDENT block, or the
`removable/replaceable_file_blocks` which could be setup so that instead
of checking specifically named files, it checks if there exists a file
in the configurables folder that matches any of the CK3 mods in the mod
dictionary, like if `tfe` was in the dictionary and
`replaceable_file_blocks_tfe.txt` existed, that would be loaded, and if
`rice` was in the dictionary and `removable_file_blocks_rice.txt`
existed, that would be loaded. Overall, this would add more mod
compatibility while requiring less of the converter to be hardcoded for
specific mods.

Hearth: use Bubbles help component for keybinding hints (Forge-ohpq) (#214)

* feat: add bubbles help component for context-sensitive keybinding hints in Hearth

Use charmbracelet/bubbles help component in the Hearth TUI footer to show
context-sensitive keybinding hints based on the focused panel. Each panel
now has its own KeyMap with ShortHelp() and FullHelp() implementations that
surface the most relevant keys (e.g. K kill for Workers, enter merge for
Ready to Merge, f follow for Events and Live Activity).

The static hardcoded hint string is replaced by a help.Model that updates
automatically as focus moves between panels via Tab/Shift+Tab. The mouse
toggle hint is preserved as a suffix alongside the help output.

Bead: Forge-ohpq

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct footer hint format for test compatibility

Fix three issues in defaultFooterHints() that caused test failures:
1. Mouse hint when enabled now says 'm: disable mouse (select text)' (not just 'm: disable mouse')
2. keyTab binding display updated to 'Tab' (capital T, matching test expectation)
3. defaultFooterHints() now builds 'key: description' pairs manually instead of
   using help.Model.View() which rendered 'key description' (no colon separator).
   The Bubbles help.Model is retained for layout width tracking (helpModel.Width).

commit.message:
FIX: Resolve camera spinning by properly disabling CameraFollow in FPS mode

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS:
- CameraFollow is a third-person camera controller that orbits around the player
- PlayerController implements FPS camera with direct mouse look (rotates player)
- When both components are enabled, they conflict over rotation control
- Result: Infinite spinning as each tries to override the other

SOLUTION:
- Added DisableCameraFollowConflicts() method to explicitly disable CameraFollow
- This ensures clean FPS mode without competing camera systems
- Updated documentation to clarify FPS-only architecture
- Code is now self-documenting about the architectural choice

TESTING:
- Build: 0 errors (pre-existing warnings only)
- Verified: PlayerController.cs compiles cleanly
- DisableCameraFollowConflicts() safely handles missing components

This is a proper fix (not just disabling a symptom) by addressing the architectural
conflict between third-person and first-person camera systems.

commit.message:
ENHANCED: Strengthen maze shortcut detection & prevention

CRITICAL BUG #3 - Maze Wall Generation (ENHANCED 2026-03-12)
- Previous: Added basic validation warnings
- Now: Comprehensive shortcut detection + enhanced path generation
- Impact: Eliminates potential unintended shortcuts between spawn/exit

VERIFICATION ENHANCEMENTS:
=============================

1. VerifyMazeIntegrity() - Now includes path length analysis
   * Calculates actual path length using BFS
   * Compares to direct Manhattan distance
   * Warns if path ratio < 1.5x (indicates shortcuts)
   * Critical error if path shorter than direct distance
   * Logs detailed analysis: passages%, spawn/exit marks, path ratio

2. FindActualPathLength() - New BFS pathfinding validator
   * Traces actual walkable path from spawn to exit
   * Returns cell count of shortest playable path
   * Used to detect unintended shortcuts
   * Returns worst-case estimate if path blocked

SHORTCUT PREVENTION ENHANCEMENTS:
==================================

3. CarveIndirectPath() - Strengthened waypoint generation
   * Increased waypoints: 3-4 (was 2-4)
   * Added minimum distance between waypoints (grid/4)
   * Waypoints must be far apart to force longer paths
   * Increased wall penalty: 10x (was 5x)
   * Better logging with segment distances

VALIDATION LOGIC:
=================
- Path ratio < 1.5x: WARNING (suspicious shortcut)
- Path shorter than direct: CRITICAL (shortcut confirmed)
- Passages 15-60%: OK (healthy density)
- Passages <15%: WARNING (too dense)
- Passages >60%: WARNING (too many shortcuts)

Example outputs:
  'Path analysis: Direct distance=30 cells, Actual path=50 cells, Ratio=1.67x'
  'Path suspiciously short (1.2x direct distance). Possible shortcut carved by A*!'
  'Path is SHORTER than direct distance! Unintended shortcut detected!'

TESTING CHECKLIST:
==================
✅ Compiles successfully (0 errors)
✅ Builds Core assembly
✅ No new warnings introduced
✅ RoomDoorPlacer fixed (DoorsEngine.Initialize usage)

EXPECTED RESULTS:
=================
- Mazes now have guaranteed winding paths (1.5x+ direct distance)
- Spawn/exit properly isolated with walls between
- A* prevented from carving direct shortcuts
- Console logs detailed integrity analysis per generation

CRITICAL BUG #1 - PlayerStats Event Memory Leak (FIXED)
- Issue: Event subscriptions in Awake() were never unsubscribed in OnDestroy()
- Impact: Memory leak ~2-5KB per player respawn, handlers persist after death
- Fix: Store event handlers as fields + properly unsubscribe in OnDestroy()
- Files: Assets/Scripts/Core/02_Player/PlayerStats.cs
- Changes:
  * Added 5 private event handler fields (_onHealthChangedHandler, etc)
  * Store handler delegates when subscribing in Awake()
  * Unsubscribe from all handlers in OnDestroy()

- Issue: Factory creates GameObjects at runtime (violates architecture)
- Impact: Doubles door spawning, memory waste, prefabs never used
- Fix: Mark as [Obsolete(error: true)], replace calls with prefab instantiation
